Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The document repeatedly describes two treatment arms: 'ADT plus docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'. This is confirmed in the abstract, methods, CONSORT diagram, Table 1, and throughout the results and discussion sections. These are the names of the experimental and control arms in the CHAARTED trial.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: The CONSORT diagram and multiple tables (e.g., Table 1, Table 2, Table 3) consistently report 397 patients in the ADT plus docetaxel (experimental) arm and 393 in the ADT alone (control) arm. These numbers are for the whole study population, not subgroups.

Control Arm - N.evidence: The number of participants in the control arm (ADT alone) is reported multiple times in the document, including in the CONSORT diagram ("Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)") and in Table 1 (column 'ADT Alone', row 'Overall', 'No. of Patients' = 393). This value represents the total number of participants included in the control arm for the whole study population.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The median follow-up duration is explicitly stated in the Results section: 'At a median follow-up of 53.7 months, the median OS was 57.6 months for the chemohormonal therapy arm versus 47.2 months for ADT alone (hazard ratio [HR], 0.72; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.89; P= .0018).' This value is also referenced in the Discussion: 'The updated analysis of the CHAARTED trial with a median follow-up of 53.7 months confirms the OS benefit from early treatment with docetaxel seen at the interim analysis with a median follow-up of 28.9 months.'

Control Arm.evidence: The control arm is consistently referred to as 'ADT alone' throughout the document, including in the abstract ('...randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel... or ADT alone'), the CONSORT diagram (Fig 1: 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)'), and all efficacy and outcome tables (e.g., Table 1: 'ADT Alone').

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus docetaxel 75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for up to six cycles without daily prednisone
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The regimen is described in the 'Patients and Methods' section: 'Eligible patients were randomly assigned at a one-to-one ratio to ADT alone versus ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for up to six cycles without daily prednisone.'

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The document describes a randomized phase III trial where 790 patients were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone. The CONSORT diagram and multiple tables (e.g., Table 1) consistently show two groups: 'ADT plus docetaxel' and 'ADT alone.' Therefore, the total count of experimental treatment arms and control arms included in the clinical trial is 2.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document does not report any results on quality of life of participants. The endpoints, tables, and figures focus on overall survival, time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, and time to clinical progression. There is no mention of quality of life assessments or results in the abstract, methods, results, or discussion sections.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The total number of participants included in the clinical trial is reported multiple times in the document. In the 'Patients and Methods' section, it states: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel ... or ADT alone.' This is confirmed in the CONSORT diagram and in the Results section: 'Between July 2006 and December 2012, 790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1), including 397 in the ADT plus docetaxel arm and 393 in the ADT alone arm.' Thus, the total number of participants is 790.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The abstract and methods sections state that patients in the first experimental arm received 'ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles.' The class of agent is therefore a combination of hormone therapy (androgen-deprivation therapy) and cytotoxic chemotherapy (docetaxel).
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The median age for the combination arm (ADT plus docetaxel, i.e., treatment) is reported in the Results section: 'The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88 years) in the combination arm...'

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The median age for the ADT alone arm (control) is reported in the Results section: '...versus 63 years (range, 39 to 91 years) in the ADT alone arm.'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Results are reported by high-volume disease throughout the document, including in Table 1, Table 2, Table 3, Figure 3, and the forest plot (Fig 4), with explicit subgroup analyses and outcomes for high-volume patients.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Results are reported by low-volume disease throughout the document, including in Table 1, Table 2, Table 3, Figure 3, and the forest plot (Fig 4), with explicit subgroup analyses and outcomes for low-volume patients.

NCT.evidence: Found in the line: 'Clinical trial information: NCT00309985.' near the beginning of the document.

Trial Name.value: CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer)
Trial Name.evidence: The full trial name is given in the title and in the abstract: 'Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial' and 'CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer)'.

Author.evidence: The author list is provided in the title section: 'Christos E. Kyriakopoulos, Yu-Hui Chen, Michael A. Carducci, Glenn Liu, David F. Jarrard, Noah M. Hahn, Daniel H. Shevrin, Robert Dreicer, Maha Hussain, Mario Eisenberger, Manish Kohli, Elizabeth R. Plimack, Nicholas J. Vogelzang, Joel Picus, Matthew M. Cooney, Jorge A. Garcia, Robert S. DiPaola, and Christopher J. Sweeney'. The 'et al.' format is appropriate for long author lists.

Year.evidence: The publication year is found at the top: 'VOLUME 36 · NUMBER 11 · APRIL 10, 2018' and 'Published at jco.org on January 31, 2018.'

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains the full text of the article, including introduction, methods, results, discussion, tables, and references, indicating it is a full publication.

Phase.evidence: Multiple mentions, e.g., in the title: 'Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial' and in the abstract: 'In this phase III study...'

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The abstract and introduction state this is a long-term survival analysis and an updated analysis of the CHAARTED trial, indicating it is a follow-up report.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: In the Results section, it states: 'In terms of tumor volume, 263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm ... had high-volume disease.' The combination arm is the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel).

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: In the Results section, it states: '... and 250 patients (63.6%) in the ADT alone arm had high-volume disease.' The ADT alone arm is the control arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: The control arm (ADT alone) had 393 patients total (see CONSORT diagram and Table 1). 250 (63.6%) had high-volume disease, so the remainder had low-volume disease: 393 - 250 = 143. This is confirmed in Table 1, which lists 'Low volume' as 143 patients in the ADT alone arm. The percentage is 143/393 = 36.4%.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: In the Results section, it states: '289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm ... were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' De novo metastatic disease is equivalent to synchronous metastases (metastases at the time of diagnosis).

In the Results section, it states: '286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' De novo metastatic disease is equivalent to synchronous metastases.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: In the Results section, it states: '289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm ... were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' The total number in the combination arm is 397, so 397 - 289 = 108 patients (27.2%) had prior local therapy (PLT), i.e., metachronous metastases.

In the Results section, it states: '286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' The total number in the ADT alone arm is 393, so 393 - 286 = 107 patients (27.2%) had prior local therapy (PLT), i.e., metachronous metastases.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: From the CONSORT diagram and study design, 397 patients were randomized to the ADT plus docetaxel arm. The protocol specified that this arm received docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles. There is no indication that any patients in this arm did not receive docetaxel as part of the treatment protocol, so 100% of the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The control arm (ADT alone, n = 393) did not receive docetaxel as per the study design, which randomized patients to either ADT plus docetaxel or ADT alone. There is no mention of docetaxel administration in the control arm before or during the trial.

Trial.value: CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer) trial
Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title ('Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial') and in the abstract ('Herein, we present the outcomes of the CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer) trial').

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document does not report any measure of quality of life, well-being, or satisfaction with health status as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey. The endpoints and results focus on overall survival, time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, and time to clinical progression, with no mention of quality of life assessments in the text, tables, or figures.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival (OS), defined as time from random assignment until death resulting from any cause.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The primary endpoint is explicitly stated in the 'Patients and Methods' section: 'The primary end point of the study was overall survival (OS), defined as time from random assignment until death resulting from any cause.' This is further supported by the focus on OS in the abstract, results, and tables/figures throughout the document.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Performance Status (PS) breakdown (number and percentage of patients with PS 0) for the treatment arm is not reported anywhere in the provided document. The tables and text do not provide PS-specific data.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Performance Status (PS) breakdown (number and percentage of patients with PS 0) for the control arm is not reported anywhere in the provided document. The tables and text do not provide PS-specific data.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Performance Status (PS) breakdown (number and percentage of patients with PS 1-2) for the treatment arm is not reported anywhere in the provided document. The tables and text do not provide PS-specific data.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Performance Status (PS) breakdown (number and percentage of patients with PS 1-2) for the control arm is not reported anywhere in the provided document. The tables and text do not provide PS-specific data.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Time to development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC), defined either serologically or clinically from the time of random assignment until PSA progression, development of worsening symptoms, evidence of radiographic progression, or patient's deterioration as per investigator's opinion; and time to clinical progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The secondary endpoints are explicitly described in the 'Study Assessments' section: 'Secondary end points included time to development of CRPC, defined either serologically or clinically from the time of random assignment until PSA progression, development of worsening symptoms, evidence of radiographic progression, or patient's deterioration as per investigator's opinion.' Additionally, time to clinical progression is reported in Table 3 and discussed in the Results and Discussion sections.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report that in the ADT plus docetaxel (treatment) arm, there were 188 deaths among 397 patients in the overall population.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report that in the ADT alone (control) arm, there were 211 deaths among 393 patients in the overall population.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 ('Time to CRPC (PSA rise or clinical progression)') reports for the overall population: 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' arm, median time to CRPC is 19.4 months (16.8 to 22.6). TTPSA is not reported as a separate endpoint, but time to CRPC (which includes PSA progression) is the closest reported measure.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: Table 2 ('Time to CRPC (PSA rise or clinical progression)') reports for the overall population: 'ADT Alone' arm, median time to CRPC is 11.7 months (10.8 to 14.4). TTPSA is not reported as a separate endpoint, but time to CRPC (which includes PSA progression) is the closest reported measure.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: The document does not report the number or percentage of patients experiencing all-cause grade 3 or higher adverse events in the treatment (ADT plus docetaxel) arm. The safety section only states that a summary of adverse effects was included in the initial publication and that no additional long-term adverse effects were observed, but no specific data are provided in this report.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients experiencing all-cause grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control (ADT alone) arm. No such data are presented in the text, tables, or figures.

The safety section refers to the initial publication for adverse event details, but no specific numbers or percentages are provided in this report.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control (ADT alone) arm. No such data are presented in this report.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related grade 5 adverse events (deaths) in the treatment (ADT plus docetaxel) arm. No such data are presented in the text, tables, or figures.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related grade 5 adverse events (deaths) in the control (ADT alone) arm. No such data are presented in this report.

ORR - N (%) | Control | CR.evidence: No data on complete response (CR) rate for the control arm is reported. The document does not mention the number or percentage of patients achieving CR in the control arm.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The abstract and methods sections state that patients were randomly assigned to receive either androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone. The combination arm is consistently referred to as 'ADT plus docetaxel' throughout the document, including in tables and figure captions. No other add-on therapy is described as part of the trial intervention.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles versus ADT alone
Type of Therapy.evidence: The abstract and 'Patients and Methods' section state that patients were randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone. This is confirmed in the CONSORT diagram and throughout the results and discussion sections.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The study is described as a 'multicenter, randomized, open-label, phase III National Cancer Institute study' in the Patients and Methods section. This indicates a randomized controlled trial (RCT) design, which is generally considered high quality for risk of bias. There is no explicit overall risk of bias judgment (e.g., 'low', 'moderate', 'high') stated in the document, but the description of the trial design provides the relevant information for the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in RCTs.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'Overall' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT plus docetaxel as 19.4 months (16.8 to 22.6).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'Overall' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT alone as 11.7 months (10.8 to 14.4).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'High volume' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT plus docetaxel as 14.9 months (12.4 to 17.2).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'High volume' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT alone as 8.6 months (6.8 to 10.5).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'Low volume' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT plus docetaxel as 31.0 months (23.1 to 51.1).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Time to CRPC) reports 'Low volume' median time to CRPC (progression-free survival) for ADT alone as 22.7 months (18.9 to 29.1).

Treatment Class.value: Chemohormonal therapy (androgen-deprivation therapy [ADT] plus docetaxel) versus ADT alone
Treatment Class.evidence: The abstract and multiple sections of the main text (including 'Patients and Methods', 'Procedures', and Table 1) clearly state that participants were randomized to receive either androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone. The term 'chemohormonal therapy' is used throughout to describe the combination of ADT plus docetaxel, and this is the classification of the treatment arms in the clinical trial.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for the overall ADT plus docetaxel arm as 57.6 months (95% CI, 52.0 to 63.9).

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for the overall ADT alone arm as 47.2 months (95% CI, 41.8 to 52.8).

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for high-volume patients in the ADT plus docetaxel arm as 51.2 months (95% CI, 45.2 to 58.1).

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for high-volume patients in the ADT alone arm as 34.4 months (95% CI, 30.1 to 42.1).

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for low-volume patients in the ADT plus docetaxel arm as 63.5 months (95% CI, 58.3 to 78.5).

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Table 1 (OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup) and the Results section both report median OS for low-volume patients in the ADT alone arm as NR (not reached), with 95% CI 59.8 to NR.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Hazard ratio for time to CRPC (progression-free survival proxy): 0.61 (95% CI, 0.52 to 0.73); Median time to CRPC: 19.4 months (16.8 to 22.6)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 reports for the overall population (all participants, regardless of metastasis type or volume) in the ADT plus docetaxel (treatment) arm: median time to CRPC (used as PFS proxy) is 19.4 months (16.8 to 22.6), with a hazard ratio of 0.61 (95% CI, 0.52 to 0.73) compared to control. No PFS rate (%) is reported, but HR and median time are provided for the whole population.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Hazard ratio for time to CRPC (progression-free survival proxy): 1 (reference); Median time to CRPC: 11.7 months (10.8 to 14.4)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 reports for the overall population (all participants, regardless of metastasis type or volume) in the ADT alone (control) arm: median time to CRPC (used as PFS proxy) is 11.7 months (10.8 to 14.4). The hazard ratio is relative to control, so control is the reference (HR=1). No PFS rate (%) is reported, but median time is provided for the whole population.
